verses.1: The LORD reigneth; let the earth rejoice; let the multitude of isles be glad.
verses.2: Clouds and darkness surround him: righteousness and judgment are the habitation of his throne.
verses.3: A fire goeth before him, and burneth up his enemies round about.
verses.4: His lightnings enlightened the world: the earth saw, and trembled.
verses.5: The hills melted like wax at the presence of the LORD, at the presence of the LORD of the whole earth.
verses.6: The heavens declare his righteousness, and all the people see his glory.
verses.7: Confounded be all they that serve graven images, that boast themselves of idols; worship him, all ye gods.
verses.8: Zion heard, and was glad; and the daughters of Judah rejoiced because of thy judgments, O LORD.
verses.9: For thou, LORD, art high above all the earth: thou art exalted far above all gods.
verses.10: Ye that love the LORD, hate evil: he preserveth the souls of his saints; he delivereth them out of the hand of the wicked.
verses.11: Light is sown for the righteous, and gladness for the upright in heart.
verses.12: Rejoice in the LORD, ye righteous; and give thanks at the remembrance of his holiness.

metadata.source: Webster's Bible (1833) — presented as-is with minor editorial corrections for the ABIDE Bible App.
